name: Temple Bar Food Market
address: Meeting House Square, Temple Bar, Dublin 2
description: One of Dublin's most beloved food markets, nestled in the cultural heart of Temple Bar. Over 30 artisan stalls offering fresh produce, baked goods, street food, and specialty Irish products.

name: Dún Laoghaire Farmers Market
description: A popular Sunday market in the leafy People's Park, featuring local producers, organic vegetables, artisan cheeses, breads, and seasonal Irish produce.

name: Marlay Park Farmers Market
description: Set against the backdrop of Marlay Park's stunning Georgian house and grounds, this Saturday market brings together the best of south Dublin's local food producers.

name: Cork English Market
address: Princes Street / Grand Parade, Cork City
description: Ireland's oldest covered market, operating since 1788. A living institution with over 140 traders selling everything from local fish and artisan cheeses to traditional tripe and drisheen.

name: Mahon Point Farmers Market
description: Cork's largest outdoor farmers market, running every Thursday with over 60 stalls of local food, crafts, and seasonal produce from County Cork and beyond.

name: Midleton Farmers Market
description: A vibrant Saturday market in the heart of East Cork, celebrated for exceptional local produce, artisan cheeses, organic vegetables, and the famous Ballymaloe region's food culture.

name: Galway City Market
address: Church Lane / Shop Street area, Galway City
description: Galway's iconic open-air market in the city centre, drawing local producers and artisans every weekend. Famous for its organic vegetables, smoked salmon, fresh flowers, and West of Ireland atmosphere.

name: Moycullen Farmers Market
description: A friendly village market on the shores of Lough Corrib, showcasing the best of Connacht's local food producers, crafts, and artisan goods.

name: Limerick Milk Market
description: One of Ireland's oldest and most atmospheric markets, operating in its covered Victorian market building since 1852. Renowned for Limerick ham, local cheeses, and Munster produce.

name: Kenmare Farmers Market
description: A charming midweek market in the picturesque town of Kenmare, featuring local Kerry produce, artisan foods, organic vegetables, and handcrafted goods.

name: Killarney Farmers Market
description: A lively Friday market in the gateway to the Ring of Kerry, celebrated for Kerry dairy products, organic meats, smoked fish, and seasonal mountain produce.

name: Ennis Farmers Market
description: Clare's most popular market, held every Friday in the historic town centre. A great source of Atlantic seafood, local cheeses, organic vegetables, and artisan breads from across the Burren region.

name: Waterford Farmers Market
description: The oldest city in Ireland hosts a wonderful Saturday market with fresh Waterford produce, artisan foods, local meats, and seasonal vegetables from the sunny South East.

name: Wexford Farmers Market
description: A well-established Friday market in the heart of the Sunny South East, featuring Wexford strawberries in season, local vegetables, artisan dairy, and fresh catch from the Celtic Sea.

name: Kilkenny Farmers Market
description: A vibrant Thursday market in the Medieval Mile, showcasing Kilkenny's outstanding food heritage — artisan cheeses, organic meats, sourdough breads, and Nore Valley produce.

name: Bray Farmers Market
description: A popular seaside market on Bray's main street, offering Wicklow Garden of Ireland produce, artisan baked goods, organic dairy, and local crafts.

name: Naas Farmers Market
description: Kildare's premier Sunday market in the county town, drawing producers from across the Liffey Valley. Known for free-range eggs, artisan breads, heritage vegetables, and local honey.

name: Navan Farmers Market
description: A Saturday market in the Royal County, showcasing the finest Boyne Valley produce — heritage potatoes, local beef, artisan cheeses, and seasonal fruit from Meath's rich farmland.

name: Westport Country Market
description: A charming Thursday market in one of Ireland's tidiest towns, offering fresh Atlantic seafood, organic Connacht vegetables, handmade jams, artisan cheeses, and local craft goods.

name: Sligo Farmers Market
description: A Saturday market in Yeats Country, featuring the finest produce from Sligo and North Connacht — wild Atlantic seafood, organic vegetables, artisan cheeses, and fresh flowers.

name: Letterkenny Farmers Market
description: Donegal's largest town hosts a wonderful Friday market celebrating the Wild Atlantic Way food culture — smoked salmon, lamb from Donegal hills, sea vegetables, and artisan baked goods.

name: Clonmel Farmers Market
description: A Saturday market in the capital of Tipperary, the food county of Ireland. Renowned for Golden Vale dairy products, Tipperary beef, apple orchards produce, and artisan craft foods.

name: Athlone Farmers Market
description: A Thursday market in the heart of Ireland, drawing producers from four provinces. The Shannon river town is known for its fresh lake fish, midlands beef, artisan dairy, and seasonal vegetables.

name: Carlow Farmers Market
description: A Saturday market in the heart of Ireland's south-east, showcasing Barrow Valley produce, artisan foods, organic meats, local dairy, and handmade preserves from County Carlow.
